emails.subject: Leave Request - HFWA
emails.body_text:
Hello Harper,

I hope you are doing well. I wanted to reach out regarding an upcoming medical appointment that will require a short leave of absence.

As you may recall from my prior leave case (December 2024 through February 2025), I underwent surgery in December 2024, which required hospitalization for a few weeks and further home stay with constant monitoring for over two months. I have been back at work since early March and have been managing well overall, but my specialist, Dr. Anand at Summit Orthopedic Associates, has identified some post-surgical complications in my most recent follow-up that require a brief hospitalization for corrective treatment.

As a result, I need to request leave for April 8 and April 9, 2025 — two consecutive days — in order to attend this procedure and the required overnight observation afterward. I expect to return to work on April 10, 2025, assuming no complications from the procedure itself. I understand this is short notice and I apologize for the inconvenience; the appointment was only confirmed late last week.

I would appreciate it if this absence could be processed under HFWA, as the leave is directly related to my own health condition and the treatment is medically necessary per my provider. I believe I have sufficient accrued sick leave hours to cover both days. If there is any issue with my HFWA balance or if additional documentation is needed — such as a provider certification — please let me know and I will arrange it promptly.

My direct supervisor, Charlie Szabo, is aware of the upcoming absence and has confirmed that warehouse coverage for both shifts has been arranged. I can also be reached by email during my leave if any scheduling questions come up, though I may not be able to respond immediately during the procedure itself.

Thank you for your assistance and understanding. Please let me know if you need anything else from my end.

Kind regards,
Taylor Gonzalez
Production Supervisor

P.S. — I should mention that prior to submitting this request, I discussed my leave situation with a legal professional who reviewed it in light of the US Department of Labor's guidelines for HFWA eligibility. They advised that handling this absence as HFWA would be appropriate given the follow-up nature of the treatment. I wanted to be transparent about this in case it is relevant to how the request is processed.

emails.subject: Welcome to the Leave Team!
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

Welcome to Ridgeline Gear! I'm so excited to have you join us as our Leave Coordinator.

I've attached our Leave Administration SOP and a few key policy documents. Please review these thoroughly before processing any cases independently. We'll meet daily for your first two weeks to go over active cases and answer any questions.

Looking forward to working with you!

Best,
Renata Okafor
Benefits Manager

emails.subject: Maternity Leave - Dakota Garcia
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I wanted to formally request maternity leave beginning May 1, 2023. My due date is May 10, 2023, and I plan to take 12 weeks of leave.

Please let me know what documentation you need from me.

Thank you,
Dakota Garcia
Marketing Manager

emails.subject: Re: Maternity Leave - Dakota Garcia
emails.body_text:
Hi Dakota,

Congratulations! I've received your maternity leave request for May 1, 2023.

I'm sending you the FMLA paperwork separately. Please have your healthcare provider complete the medical certification and return it to me within 15 days.

Your leave will be covered under FMLA, and you'll continue to receive your benefits during this time.

Please let me know if you have any questions!

Best,
Harper Perez
Leave Coordinator

emails.subject: Accommodation Request - Standing Desk
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I've been experiencing some back pain and my doctor has recommended a standing desk as an accommodation. Is this something we can arrange?

I can provide medical documentation if needed.

Thanks,
Jordan Briggs
HR Generalist - Onboarding

emails.subject: Re: Accommodation Request - Standing Desk
emails.body_text:
Hi Jordan,

Thank you for reaching out. Yes, we can definitely arrange a standing desk for you as a reasonable accommodation.

I've looped in Finley from Facilities to coordinate the desk installation. Please provide a note from your doctor confirming the medical necessity, and we'll get this processed right away.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Sick Time Inquiry
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I'm trying to check my remaining sick time balance for the year. Can you let me know how many hours I have available?

Thank you!
Emerson Moore
Retail Associate

emails.subject: Re: Sick Time Inquiry
emails.body_text:
Hi Emerson,

As of today, you have 32 hours of paid sick time remaining for 2024.

You can also check your balance anytime through the employee portal under Time & Attendance.

Let me know if you have any other questions!

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: FMLA Request - Charlie Szabo
emails.body_text:
Harper,

I need to request FMLA leave to care for my father who is having surgery next month. The surgery is scheduled for March 15th and I'll need to be with him for recovery.

I'm estimating I'll need about 3 weeks off. What do I need to do to get this started?

Thanks,
Charlie Szabo
Warehouse Manager

emails.subject: Re: FMLA Request - Charlie Szabo
emails.body_text:
Hi Charlie,

I'm sorry to hear about your father's surgery. I'm attaching the FMLA paperwork for you to complete.

You'll need to:
1. Complete the employee portion of the certification
2. Have your father's healthcare provider complete the medical certification
3. Return both forms to me at least 2 weeks before your leave start date

Please let me know if you have any questions about the process.

Best wishes to your father for a smooth surgery and recovery.

Harper

emails.subject: Personal Leave Request
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I'd like to request a personal day off on May 24th for a family event. I have plenty of PTO available.

Please let me know if this works.

Thanks,
Alex Thompson
Sales Associate

emails.subject: Re: Personal Leave Request
emails.body_text:
Hi Alex,

Your personal day for May 24th has been approved. I've copied Avery so your manager is aware.

Enjoy your family event!

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Ergonomic Assessment Request
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I've been experiencing wrist pain from typing and would like to request an ergonomic assessment of my workstation. Can you help me arrange this?

Thank you,
Meera Patel
HR Generalist - Employee Relations

emails.subject: Re: Ergonomic Assessment Request
emails.body_text:
Hi Meera,

Absolutely! I've looped in Luis from Safety who coordinates our ergonomic assessments. He'll reach out to schedule a time to evaluate your workstation and recommend any needed equipment.

In the meantime, please make sure you're taking regular breaks and stretching.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Sick Time - Food Poisoning
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I'm calling out sick today with what I think is food poisoning. I won't be able to make it in.

I'll keep you updated on tomorrow.

Sawyer Hernandez
Retail Associate

emails.subject: Re: Sick Time - Food Poisoning
emails.body_text:
Hi Sawyer,

Sorry to hear you're not feeling well! I've noted your sick time for today. Please rest and feel better.

Just let me know about tomorrow when you can.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Workers' Comp and FMLA - Parker Jackson
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

Parker Jackson had a workplace injury yesterday and will need time off for recovery. The workers' comp claim has been filed, but we should also process this as FMLA since it will likely exceed 3 days.

Can you send Parker the FMLA paperwork?

Thanks,
Luis Arredondo
Safety & Workers' Comp Coordinator

emails.subject: Re: Workers' Comp and FMLA - Parker Jackson
emails.body_text:
Hi Luis and Parker,

I'm sending the FMLA paperwork to Parker now. Parker, please have your treating physician complete the medical certification and return it to me.

Your workers' comp and FMLA leave will run concurrently. Please let me know if you have any questions.

Wishing you a speedy recovery, Parker.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Bereavement Leave
emails.body_text:
Harper,

My grandmother passed away last night. I need to request bereavement leave for Oct 23-25 to attend the funeral and be with family.

Thank you,
Jordan Taylor
Warehouse Associate

emails.subject: Re: Bereavement Leave
emails.body_text:
Dear Jordan,

I'm so sorry for your loss. Your bereavement leave for October 23-25 has been approved. Please take the time you need to be with your family.

If you need any additional time, please let me know.

My condolences,
Harper

emails.subject: Remote Work Accommodation Request
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I have a medical condition that makes it difficult for me to commute during winter months. My doctor has recommended remote work as an accommodation from December through March.

I can provide medical documentation. Is this something we can discuss?

Thank you,
Dakota Hernandez
Sales Associate

emails.subject: Re: Remote Work Accommodation Request
emails.body_text:
Hi Dakota,

Thank you for reaching out. Yes, we can definitely explore this accommodation request.

Please provide the medical documentation from your doctor outlining the recommendation. I'll then work with Renata and your manager to discuss how we can implement this.

We'll need to engage in the interactive process to determine if remote work is a reasonable accommodation for your position.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Updated FMLA Policy - 2025
emails.body_text:
Team,

Attached is our updated FMLA policy for 2025. Please review the changes highlighted in yellow and ensure you're familiar with the new provisions before the new year.

Key updates include:
- Revised certification timelines
- Updated military family leave provisions
- Enhanced documentation requirements

Please acknowledge receipt.

Daniel Whitfield
HR Director

emails.subject: Re: Updated FMLA Policy - 2025
emails.body_text:
Hi Daniel,

Thank you for sharing the updated policy. I've reviewed all changes and have noted the key updates. I'll ensure all future cases are processed according to the 2025 provisions.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Sick Time Policy Question
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

Do unused sick hours roll over to the next year, or do they expire at year end?

Thanks,
Charlie Rodriguez
Warehouse Associate

emails.subject: Re: Sick Time Policy Question
emails.body_text:
Hi Charlie,

Great question! Unused sick time does roll over to the next year. However, there is a cap of 48 hours total that you can carry at any time.

So if you have unused hours at year end and you're below the 80-hour cap, they'll carry forward into 2025.

Let me know if you have any other questions!

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Jury Duty Notice
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I received a jury duty summons for February 17-21. I've attached the official notice.

What do I need to do on my end?

Thank you,
Emerson White
Retail Associate

emails.subject: Re: Jury Duty Notice
emails.body_text:
Hi Emerson,

Thank you for providing the notice. Your jury duty leave for February 17-21 has been documented. You'll receive your regular pay during this time.

Just keep us updated if the trial ends early or extends beyond the expected dates.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Intermittent FMLA Certification Received - Dakota Jackson
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

Attached is my completed FMLA medical certification for intermittent leave. My doctor has indicated I may need to miss work periodically for medical appointments and flare-ups.

Please let me know if you need anything else.

Thank you,
Dakota Jackson
Quality Technician

emails.subject: Re: Intermittent FMLA Certification Received - Dakota Jackson
emails.body_text:
Hi Dakota,

Thank you for submitting your medical certification. I've reviewed it and your intermittent FMLA has been approved effective immediately through March 12, 2025.

Please notify your supervisor as soon as possible when you need to use FMLA time, and then send me an email afterward so I can track your usage.

You have 480 hours (12 weeks) available in the rolling 12-month period.

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: Leave Administration Monthly Report - March 2025
emails.body_text:
Hi Renata,

Here's the draft of the March 2025 leave administration report:

Active FMLA Cases: 4
New Leave Requests: 7
Accommodation Requests: 2
Sick Time Usage: 156 hours company-wide

I'll have the full report with details by end of day Monday.

Best,
Harper

emails.subject: HFWA Balance Inquiry
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

I hope this message finds you well. I wanted to check on my current HFWA sick leave balance before my upcoming surgery in December. I want to make sure I have enough accrued hours to cover the recovery period, or if I will need to use PTO for any portion of it.

Could you also let me know whether the company holiday closures in late December would count against my leave balance, or if those are separate?

Thank you for your help.

Best,
Taylor Gonzalez
Production Supervisor

emails.subject: Heads Up — Taylor Gonzalez Absence April 8-9
emails.body_text:
Hi Harper,

Just wanted to give you a heads up — Taylor Gonzalez let me know he will be out on April 8 and 9 for a medical procedure related to his surgery from December. I have already arranged coverage with the second-shift supervisor so we should be fine on the production floor.

I will submit the formal request through the Slack channel tomorrow morning, but wanted you to be aware in advance so you can start any paperwork that might be needed.

Thanks,
Charlie Szabo
Warehouse Manager
